title: Part 7 – Passage unique : inscription à l'assurance santé
instructions: Lisez la note de service, puis répondez aux questions.
passage:
To: All Staff
From: Benefits Office
Subject: Annual Health Insurance Enrollment

The annual open enrollment period for health insurance begins on November 1st and ends on November 30th. During this time, employees can change their health plan, add or remove dependents, or enroll in additional coverage such as dental and vision insurance.

If you do not make any changes during this period, your current coverage will automatically continue for next year. However, employees who want to switch from the Basic Plan to the Premium Plan must submit a request form, available on the company intranet, before November 25th.

A representative from our insurance provider will be available in the main conference room on November 10th and 17th, from 1 p.m. to 4 p.m., to answer questions in person. Employees may also schedule a phone consultation by emailing benefits@company.com.

We encourage everyone to review their current coverage carefully, as plan details and premiums may change from year to year.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of the memo?
choices: To announce a new health insurance provider
To inform employees about the open enrollment period
To cancel the dental insurance plan
To request employee feedback on benefits
questions.explanation: La note informe les employés de la période d'inscription ouverte aux assurances santé.

questions.prompt: What happens if an employee makes no changes during the enrollment period?
choices: They will be switched to the Basic Plan
They must contact the Benefits Office immediately
questions.explanation: "your current coverage will automatically continue for next year".

questions.prompt: What must employees do before November 25th to switch to the Premium Plan?
questions.explanation: "must submit a request form, available on the company intranet, before November 25th".

questions.prompt: How can employees schedule a phone consultation?
choices: By visiting the main conference room
By submitting a form to their manager
questions.explanation: "Employees may also schedule a phone consultation by emailing benefits@company.com".
